Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Continued To Apr-15-26 At 01:00 P.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KATHERINE BAXTER'S DEMURRER

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Katherine Baxter (previously Doe No. 1) demurs to specified causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Anyssa Lerio and Dionna Ruiz. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract and as Lerio's third cause of action for negligence. The demurrer is otherwise overruled. (There is a discrepancy between the numbering of the causes of action in the parties' briefs and that found in the Complaint itself. The court uses the numbers found in the Complaint.)

The first cause of action is for breach of contract. Plaintiffs do not oppose Baxter's demurrer to this cause of action. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

The third cause of action is for negligence. Baxter contends that this cause of action sounds in medical negligence and duplicates the second cause of action for professional negligence. Lerio does not oppose the demurrer as to her third cause of action for negligence. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to Lerio's negligence claim. As to Ruiz's, she may plead her case in the alternative. The demurrer is overruled as to Ruiz's ordinary negligence cause of action.

The fourth cause of action is Ruiz's claim for conversion against Baxter, alleging interference with her embryo. "Conversion is the wrongful exercise of dominion over the property of another. The elements of a conversion are the plaintiff's ownership or right to possession of the property at the time of the conversion; the defendant's conversion by a wrongful act or disposition of property rights; and damages." (Oakdale Vill. Grp. v. Fong, 43 Cal.App.4th 539, 543-544.) Ruiz adequately alleges that Baxter interfered with her possession of her embryo by implanting it in Lerio without Ruiz's consent, to Ruiz's damage. (Compl., paras. 56-59.)

The fifth cause of action is for violations of the unfair competition law, Business & Professions Code, Sec. 17200. The UCL is broad and its remedies reach the conduct of individuals. (Id., Sec. 17203 ["Any person who engages, has engaged, or proposes to engage in unfair competition may be enjoined in any court of competent jurisdiction."].) The court concludes the claim is adequately pled.

The sixth cause of action is for violations of the California Legal Remedies Act. This cause of action was dismissed on July 24, 2025, before the amendment to substitute Baxter as a Doe. This cause of action is out of the case and the demurrer is overruled as moot to this cause of action.

The seventh cause of action is for battery. The complaint adequately pleads that Baxter participated in performing a medical procedure that was different than what was consented to. The demurrer is overruled.

The eighth cause of action is for a violation of Penal Code Sec. 367g, which states it is "unlawful for anyone to use an embryo in assisted reproduction technology for any purpose other than indicated by the embryo provider's signature on a written consent form." Taking the allegations of the Complaint as true, plaintiffs have adequately alleged that Baxter used an embryo for a purpose other than what was consented to. The demurrer to this cause of action is overruled.

Proposed order submitted is under review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2024 Set For Trial On Mar-17-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MAIYA RODDICK-FULLER, Esq. of Hall Prangle & Schoonveld, LLC (Tel: 760-810-4445) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT REPRODUCTIVE MEDICINE ASSOCIATES OF NORTHERN Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is overruled.
The complaint pleads that plaintiffs are a female couple who sought to start a family via in vitro fertilization - one child with each plaintiff's DNA. However, for the second pregnancy, defendants allegedly implanted the wrong embryo, resulting in two children with the same plaintiff's DNA.
Defendants argue that plaintiffs are limited to their claim for medical negligence; to find otherwise, defendants say, would allow evasion of the Medical Injury Compensation Reform Act's cap on pain-and-suffering damages in medical malpractice cases. Plaintiffs rely largely on Johnson v. Open Door Community Health Centers (2017) 15 Cal.App.5th 153, defendants on Flores v. Presbyterian Intercommunity Hospital (2016) 63 Cal.4th 75. The two opinions are not readily reconciled. But both show that a MICRA exclusivity analysis is fact intensive and fact specific. The issue will be best addressed after fact discovery.
The complaint's non-medical-negligence claims otherwise suffice for the pleading stage:
Breach of contract. (Cmplt. 6:6-7:13; CACI 303, 304.) The contract need not be quoted or attached to the complaint.
Negligence. (Cmplt. 8:14-9:17; CACI 400.) Theories may be pled alternatively. (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.)
Conversion. (Cmplt. 9:18-10:14; CACI 2100.)
UCL. (Cmplt. 10:15-12:22.) Unlawful, unfair or fraudulent business acts or practices are adequately pled. (Id.)
CLRA. (Cmplt. 13:1-14:12, 2:1-12:22.) A claim is asserted with adequate particularity. (Id.)
Medical Battery. (Cmplt. 14:13-15:11; CACI 530A.)
Penal Code 367. (Cmplt. 15:12-16:3.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (Via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JAMIE G. GOLDSTEIN, Esq. of Arias Sanguinetti Wang & Team LLP (Tel: 510-629-4877) for Plaintiffs;
Atty. JONQUIL L. WHITEHEAD, Esq. of Hall Prangle & Schoonveld, LLC (Tel: 760-810-4445) for Defendants.

Hearing RE: DEFENDANTS REPRODUCTIVE MEDICINE ASSOCIATES OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA PC, AND DANIEL KASER's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling to take this matter off calendar. The Whitehead Declaration does not show parties met and conferred in person, by telephone or by video conference, as required by CCP 430.41(a).

The Court indicates to defense counsel that she may file an appropriate declaration which outlines how the parties complied with CCP 430.41(a) and may re-notice defendant's demurrer for a hearing date of 2/13/24 or later.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court Reporter: Estrella Herman, CSR 13865 (Tel: 562-822-4296/E-mail: e.hermanCSR@gmail.com) (302/RBU)
